acceptance.assertion: The SOPS-backed ntfy topic materializer fsyncs the containing directory after create-only publication and after atomic replacement so a reported success is crash-durable at the filesystem metadata boundary.
Private-file no-symlink reads, 0600 materialization, secret-free error output, create-only no-overwrite semantics and the existing dispatcher runtime interface remain unchanged.
Focused tests cover directory-sync success and failure behavior; the exact diff is reviewed and required CI is green before merge.

approval.note: Registered from the T045 closeout because the reviewed SOPS helper is correct for confidentiality and atomic replacement but does not yet fsync parent-directory metadata for crash-durable publication. The user explicitly authorized completing and registering useful residual work.

goal: Close the remaining crash-durability gap in the SOPS-backed ntfy topic materializer by durably syncing directory metadata after create-only publication and atomic replacement without changing the dispatcher runtime contract or exposing secret material.

does_not_establish: permission to alter private age key storage
permission to change the ntfy dispatcher runtime contract

rollback.strategy: Revert only the directory-sync hardening commit. The existing SOPS ciphertext, runtime materialization and dispatcher contract remain valid and independently recoverable.

title: Harden SOPS ntfy materialization crash durability
